debates.question: What does the death of Aaron the high priest signify?

sides.position: The passing of the first priesthood - the high priest himself dying outside the land, the Aaronic priesthood mortal and 'not suffered to continue by reason of death' (Hebrews 7:23), a figure of the weakness of the legal priesthood that pointed to Christ the great High Priest who continues ever and has an unchangeable priesthood (Hebrews 7:24-25)

schools.summary: The extent of the camp by Jordan in the plains of Moab: 'and they pitched by Jordan, from Bethjesimoth even unto Abelshittim in the plains of Moab' — the great host spread along the Jordan, from Beth-jesimoth to Abel-shittim (the they pitched by Jordan, the camp by the river, the from Bethjesimoth even unto Abelshittim, the extent of the encampment, the in the plains of Moab, the place, the figure, the vast camp of God's people spread along the Jordan, the host arrived at the border, the truth that the whole congregation, brought through the wilderness, encamped at the edge of the land, the figure of the people of God gathered at the threshold of the inheritance, the river before them, the place of the last events of Numbers, the laws given and the conquest charged, the end of the journeys, Numbers 25:1); the camp spread along the Jordan (the extent, Numbers 25:1) — the extent (the great camp); 'in the plains of Moab' (the place of the encampment at the border, the threshold of the land, the figure, the people gathered at the edge of the inheritance, the providence, the camp, the charge to take the land to follow, vv.50-56) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

schools.summary: The LORD speaking concerning the taking of the land: 'and the LORD spake unto Moses in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho, saying' — the LORD spoke to Moses at the border, now to charge Israel concerning the conquest and division of the land (vv.51-56, the the LORD spake unto Moses, God's command, the in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho, at the threshold of the land, the figure, God charging his people at the edge of the inheritance, the conquest and division commanded, the truth that God directs his people at every step, now at the border with the law of the conquest, the figure of the word of God that goes before the people into the land, the charge to take and to purge it, the command given at the threshold, before the crossing of Jordan, God speaking at the plains of Moab, showing his hand directing the conquest of the land); the LORD speaking to Moses (the charge concerning the land) — the new section (the conquest); 'in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho' (the threshold of the land, the charge at the border, the figure, the word going before into the land, the providence, the command, the charge to drive out to follow, vv.51-56) — Gill.

schools.summary: The charge to be given when Israel passes over Jordan: 'speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye are passed over Jordan into the land of Canaan' — the charge looks forward to the day Israel would cross Jordan into the land (the speak unto the children of Israel, the word to the people, the when ye are passed over Jordan into the land of Canaan, the crossing into the land, the figure, the looking forward to the entrance into the land, the crossing of Jordan, the truth that God's charge concerning the conquest is set before the people while yet on the border, the figure of the entrance into the inheritance, the Jordan to be crossed, the land to be possessed, the command given beforehand, that the people know God's will for the land they were about to enter, Joshua 3:17); the charge to the people (the command) — the word (to Israel); 'when ye are passed over Jordan into the land of Canaan' (the crossing into the land, the entrance, Joshua 3:17, the figure, the entrance into the inheritance, the providence, the charge, the driving out of the inhabitants to follow, vv.52) — Gill.

schools.summary: The charge to drive out the inhabitants and destroy all their idolatry: 'then ye shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and destroy all their pictures, and destroy all their molten images, and quite pluck down all their high places' — the purging — 'ye shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and destroy all their pictures, and destroy all their molten images, and quite pluck down all their high places': Israel was to dispossess the Canaanites and utterly destroy every object and place of their idolatry (the witnesses noting the significance — the total purging of the land from idolatry, the inhabitants driven out, and all the instruments of false worship — the carved figures, the molten images, the high places — utterly destroyed, that no snare of idolatry remain, the lesson — that God's people must put away all idolatry without compromise, the command not merely to drive out the people but to destroy the very objects of their false worship, lest the people be ensnared, vv.55, the thoroughness of the command, 'destroy... destroy... quite pluck down,' no idol spared, the truth that God will have his people wholly separate from idols, the land cleansed for his worship, the figure — the call to put away every idol from the heart and life, 'little children, keep yourselves from idols,' 1 John 5:21, 'what agreement hath the temple of God with idols?... come out from among them, and be ye separate,' 2 Corinthians 6:16-17, the figure of the believer's mortification of every false love and the cleansing of the heart from all that rivals God, the truth that half-measures with idolatry are forbidden, the idols to be utterly destroyed, the figure of the purging of the land and the heart from all idolatry, the figure of the total destruction of idols, that God's people be wholly his); the Canaanites to be driven out before Israel (the dispossession, the land cleared) — the driving out (the inhabitants); the utter destruction of every idol, image, and high place, that no snare of idolatry remain (the purging of the land, the separation from idols, 1 John 5:21; 2 Corinthians 6:16-17) — the figure (the putting away of every idol from the heart, the providence, the charge, the dwelling in the land to follow, vv.53) — Calvin, Ellicott, Wesley, Gill.

debates.question: What does the charge to destroy all their idols and high places require?

sides.position: The total purging of the land from idolatry - the inhabitants driven out and every object and place of false worship utterly destroyed, no compromise with idols, a figure of the believer's call to put away every idol from the heart and be wholly separate to God (1 John 5:21; 2 Corinthians 6:16-17)

the inhabitants dispossessed and every object and place of their false worship utterly destroyed, that no snare remain

schools.summary: The land given to Israel to possess: 'and ye shall dispossess the inhabitants of the land, and dwell therein: for I have given you the land to possess it' — Israel was to dispossess the Canaanites and dwell in the land, for God had given it to them (the ye shall dispossess the inhabitants of the land, and dwell therein, the possession, the for I have given you the land to possess it, the gift, the ground of the possession, the figure, the land given by God, to be possessed by his gift, the truth that the inheritance was God's gift, the possession resting on his promise, not on their own strength, the figure of the inheritance given of God, possessed by faith in his gift, the truth that what God has given his people they are to take and hold, the land theirs by his word, Genesis 13:15, the gift that grounds the conquest, the possession of the promise); Israel dispossessing the foe and dwelling (the possession) — the possession (dwelling in the land); 'I have given you the land to possess it' (the land God's gift, the ground of the possession, Genesis 13:15, the figure, the inheritance given of God, the providence, the gift, the division by lot to follow, vv.54) — Gill, Calvin.

schools.summary: The division of the land by lot for an inheritance: 'and ye shall divide the land by lot for an inheritance among your families: and to the more ye shall give the more inheritance, and to the fewer ye shall give the less inheritance: every man's inheritance shall be in the place where his lot falleth; according to the tribes of your fathers ye shall inherit' — the lot — 'ye shall divide the land by lot for an inheritance among your families: and to the more ye shall give the more inheritance, and to the fewer ye shall give the less... every man's inheritance shall be in the place where his lot falleth': the land divided by lot among the tribes and families, the larger tribes receiving more, the smaller less, each portion fixed by the lot (the witnesses noting the significance — the inheritance distributed by the lot, that is, by God's own appointment, the lot owning that the division was not by man's will or favour but by God, 'the lot is cast into the lap; but the whole disposing thereof is of the LORD,' Proverbs 16:33, the lesson — that God himself assigns to each his portion in the inheritance, the lot the token of God's sovereign distribution, each tribe and family receiving the place God appointed, the size by their number but the place by the lot, the truth that the inheritance of God's people is of his appointing, every man's portion fixed by him, the figure — the inheritance of the saints distributed by God, 'who hath made us meet to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light,' Colossians 1:12, the figure of the lot that gives each his place, a token of the providence of God who appoints to every one his portion, 'the lines are fallen unto me in pleasant places; yea, I have a goodly heritage,' Psalm 16:5-6, the truth that God's people receive their inheritance not by their own choosing but by his sovereign gift, the figure of the portion appointed by God, the inheritance divided by the lot of his providence, the figure of the inheritance distributed by God's own appointment to each his portion); the land divided by lot among the families (the inheritance by lot, God's appointment, Proverbs 16:33) — the lot (the division); the size by number, the place by the lot, each portion fixed by God (the inheritance of God's appointing, Colossians 1:12; Psalm 16:5-6) — the figure (the inheritance distributed by God to each his portion, the providence, the division, the warning of the remnant to follow, vv.55) — Calvin, Keil, Gill.

debates.question: What does dividing the land by lot signify?

sides.position: The inheritance distributed not by man's will but by the lot, that is, by God's own sovereign disposing (Proverbs 16:33) - each tribe and family receiving the place he appointed, a figure of the inheritance of the saints distributed by God (Colossians 1:12) and the portion he appoints to every one of his people (Psalm 16:5-6)

themes: And ye shall divide the land by lot for an inheritance among your families... every man's inheritance shall be in the place where his lot falleth; according to the tribes of your fathers ye shall inherit

schools.summary: The warning that the inhabitants left undriven would be pricks and thorns: 'but if ye will not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you; then it shall come to pass, that those which ye let remain of them shall be pricks in your eyes, and thorns in your sides, and shall vex you in the land wherein ye dwell' — the peril of compromise — 'if ye will not drive out the inhabitants of the land... then those which ye let remain of them shall be pricks in your eyes, and thorns in your sides, and shall vex you in the land wherein ye dwell': if Israel spared any of the Canaanites, the remnant would become a constant torment and snare (the witnesses noting the significance — the warning against incomplete obedience, the sparing of what God commanded to destroy, the inhabitants left undriven becoming pricks in the eyes and thorns in the sides, a perpetual vexation and a snare to idolatry, vv.56, the lesson — the peril of compromise with sin, the danger of sparing what God bids us destroy, the half-obedience that leaves the enemy, which then becomes the believer's torment, the truth that the sin or idol spared will turn and wound, 'thorns in your sides,' the besetting trouble, the figure — the danger of sparing any sin, the idol or lust not wholly mortified becoming a snare and a grief, 'if thy right eye offend thee, pluck it out,' Matthew 5:29, the figure of the besetting sin that vexes the soul because it was not put away, the truth, fulfilled in Israel's history, that the Canaanites they spared became their snare and ruin, Judges 2:3, 'they shall be as thorns in your sides, and their gods shall be a snare unto you,' the figure of the peril of incomplete obedience, the spared enemy turned tormentor, the warning that what is not utterly put away will pierce and vex, the figure of the danger of sparing what God commands to destroy, the remnant become thorns and pricks); debates.question: What does the warning of 'pricks in your eyes and thorns in your sides' teach?

schools.summary: The warning that God would judge Israel as the nations if they sinned like them: 'moreover it shall come to pass, that I shall do unto you, as I thought to do unto them' — if Israel imitated the Canaanites in their idolatry, God would do to Israel what he purposed against the nations (the I shall do unto you, the judgment on Israel, the as I thought to do unto them, as God purposed against the Canaanites, the figure, the solemn warning that God is no respecter of persons, and would judge his own people if they sinned like the nations, the truth that the privilege of being God's people is no shield to those who do the abominations of the heathen, 'you only have I known of all the families of the earth: therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities,' Amos 3:2, the figure of the impartial justice of God, who would cast out his own people for the sins for which he cast out the Canaanites, fulfilled in Israel's later captivity, the warning that closes the chapter, that Israel not presume on their election but obey); the judgment threatened on Israel (the warning) — the judgment (on Israel); 'as I thought to do unto them' (as God purposed against the Canaanites, the impartial justice, Amos 3:2, the figure, God judging his own people for the sins of the nations, the providence, the warning, the borders of the land to follow, ch.34) — Gill, Calvin.
